feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Marketing Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Marketing Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Marketing Cloud email campaign email opened but no link clicked, behavior triggers
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to setup an email campaign in SAP Marketing Cloud and I want to setup a follow-up email if a customer opened an email but did not click any links (after a period of time)

How would I go about this? Does the click through action, with the No branch, imply that the customer opened the email but did not click any links?

Kind Regards,

Felix

entry.title: How to send Emails with Attachments in SAP Marketing Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a requirement, where the Business want to send the Bulk Emails to the Customers with Attachment's in SAP Marketing Cloud. I did not find any option to assign attachments, Can you please suggest is this out of box functionality or not.

 

Regards,

Hemanth.B

entry.title: Execute custom logic at permission import
entry.content:
Our business is uploading permission data via the "Data File Load" app. We noticed that in some cases the contact IDs contain upper case characters, however, all IDs in the SAP Marketing Cloud are stored in lower case. It seems as this is an issue and the Marketing Cloud is case-sensitive in this case as the import monitor shows an error "No contact found for ID SAP_HYBRIS_CONSUMER...". Of course, the business can make sure to always upload the IDs in lower case. However, if possible, I would like to implement some custom logic to make the contact IDs always lower case before the further execution takes place. I tried several BadIs but it seems as none of them is executed at permission upload - at least the custom logic trace is not logging anything. 

  • CUAN_IMPORT_IC
  • CUAN_FIND_INTERACTION_CONTACT
  • CUAN_IMPORT_IA_4_IC
  • CUAN_IA_REVISE_FOR_IMPORT

Did someone already implement custom logic at permission upload and can point me to the right direction? 

Best
Christian

entry.title: sap-outbound-id in a different url parameter
entry.content:
Hi,

In content studio, when you toggle "Outbound ID for Consuming App", this appends the url parameter sap-outbound-id .

You can also use your own "name of link parameter" with "value of link parameter" to add url parameters with the name of your choice.

I would require a link parameter named "cf1" with the sap-outbound-id as its value.

How can I achieve this? I do not seem to find the outbound id in the list of fields for the "value of link parameter"...

Context is that I want to do a survey with Pointerpro. Pointerpro can recognize url parameters, but they need to be in parameters called cf1, cf2, cf3, ... . The url parameter "sap-outbound-id" is not recognized by Pointerpro. 

I looked in the custom logic "Adjust Hyperlink Parameters While Sending Emails" but there it seems mostly about the google analytics parameters, and I also don't see at first sigh a variable that contains the outbound id, but I'm not sure about that.

 

Thanks for your advice,

Kr

Joyca

entry.title: How to Migrate on-premise SAP marketing cloud data to any other cloud system?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are using SAP Hybris Marketing cloud on-prem and we want to migrate all the data & permission present in the system to some other cloud product.

Can anyone suggest what is the easy way to do it?

We were thinking of writing a program in backend (ABAP) and then extract the data in files. But there is millions of data present so this should be the last option if we are not able to find any easy solution.

Any lead would be helpful.

Thank you!

entry.title: trigger-based email campaign for contact-to-account relationship
entry.content:
Hi all,

we have activated the contact-to-account relationship feature and the C4 integration. Now we would like to trigger campaigns for the relationship instead of the contact (the relationship email address should be used instead of the contact email address).

As example:

Contact A has a relationship to Account X and Account Y.

Contact A has email 123@email.com

Relationship Contact A to Account X has email 456@email.com

Relationship Contact A to Account Y has email 123@email.com

Now if we load an interaction for Contact A (using the SAP_C4C_BUPA Origin) and also with the Account X as InteractionAdditionalInteractionContacts the email triggered is being sent to 123@email.com which is wrong since this is the email on the Contact record and it changes every time we change the email in one of the relationships on C4.

Do you know a way to archive this and sent the email using the right email address? 

We are using a campaign without communication category and ignoring marketing permissions.

Thanks in advance!

Regards

Klaus

entry.title: Wrong KPIs with SAP Marketing Cloud Landing Pages?
entry.content:
Hello SAP-community,

our customer created a form, embedded it in an landingpage, exported the HTML and published it with SAP Marketing Cloud. The exported html then was hosted externally.

After finishing multiple LinkedIn campaigns, aiming with all advertisments at the same URL (=the one of the hosted landingpage), the following effect was observed:

  • Now, comparing the KPIs of SMC and LinkedIn there is a high difference
  • (provided by SMC standard in Content Studio and LinkedIn Ads Manager) The KPIs of LinkedIn -> the clicks to the landingpage are 20x higher than the tracked views of the landing page.

Which effect are we missing here? It would be great to understand how SMC counts its views and other metrics for the forms/landingpages.

I thought that it may have something to do with the fact that the website is exported and hosted externally, but not sure yet. At least after publishing the landing page in SMC Content Studio, visiting it via the automatically generated SMC-URL and subscribing via the embedded form, works perfectly and is also counted correctly.

I will also update this post, if my tests are done.

I would be very thankful for your ideas!

Many thanks in advance,
Best regards, Stefan

entry.title: Microsoft Outlook (version 2405) rendering issues
entry.content:
Hi, 

Has anyone had any issues with the New Microsoft Outlook (version 2405). We've noticed that the rendering is poor, with spacing and button placement completely different to that shown in the preview in Content Studio? I'd say this is solely a Microsoft issue but strangely the same email sent from our different emailing tool (Spotler) renders as expected. Interested to know if anyone else has had the issue. 

Thanks,
Chris

entry.title: SMS for SAP Marketing Cloud - how to activate?
entry.content:
Hi,

We'd like to activate the SMS channel on our SAP Marketing Cloud.

What are the first steps here? Do we need to contact our SAP account manager to activate additional licenses, or can we go straight to Sinch and get a contract with them directly and then just set up the communication arrangements etc?

Thanks,

Joyca

entry.title: trigger-based campaign ignore interactions email address
entry.content:
Hi all,

we have to trigger a campaign with a custom interaction type. We are using an email address as id and EMAIL as id origin of the interaction.

The system uses a different email address as the email address in the trigger interaction. 

We use the ignore marketing permissions and we have to have control of which email address will be used in the campaign execution since the user triggers this campaign by itself in a customer portal and can choose an which email address from all the stored email address in his profile he wants to use.

Is there a possibility to archive this? 

We have the following problem: the customer can delete his email address on the customer portal but there will not be deleted on MC since add id cannot be deleted and when the user triggers the campaign for a new email address the MC campaign still uses the old (deleted) one.

Thanks in advance and regards

Klaus

entry.title: SAP Marketing Cloud top interest in segmentation
entry.content:
Dear experts,

does anybody have an idea of how to determine the "top interest" of a contact in segmentation?

Background/Use Case: 

A contact has multiple interactions with different interests assigned, e.g.

  • 10 interactions with interest A
  • 7 interactions with interest B
  • 20 interactions with interest C

Marketing wants to send out an email based on the "top interest" of the contact. So the contact should only receive one email (not three). In our case the email with the content for interest C.

Using segmentation we could only determine which contacts have which interest items and could use "Count of Interactions". But that would not solve our requirement.

Happy for any ideas.

Kind Regards

Tobias

entry.title: Add a title in BadI Open Channel
entry.content:
Hello,

We have implemented the BadI "open Channel: Enhance Payload for Data Transfer" in this, We have added the fields that are used to send the information, but in the campaigns view, I want to add a division of the information so that it has more order.

Does anyone know if I can add this "title" or "division" in BadI, itself and how I would ad the code?

entry.title: Lock content while campaign status is "In Approval"/"Released" for 4-eye principle approval workflow
entry.content:
Hello CX Community

We have a special request and hope that there are some of you who can help us further.

For audit reasons, we have to introduce a four-eye principle check in the Marketing Cloud. Before sending an email campaign, the approver must check the content which someone else (therefore 4-eye) has created. 

So far we have used the normal approval workflow to approve or reject the campaigns.
One problem, however, is that the content of the campaign can still be changed by any user during the approval process (In Approval) or even afterwards (Released). We want to prevent this.

We are currently thinking about two possible scenarios:

1.) While the Campaign is in its various approvals phases, the content is being “locked” so that it can no longer be changed. In the best case scenario, the mailing cannot be changed even after it has been sent.

2.) We are able to extend the normal campaign approval workflow to the object "content" from the content studio. That there is also a two step approval for releasing the messages. 


Basically, it can be said that it is more about the content than about the target groups or campaign execution details. The content of each campaign must be reviewed and approved. After that, no more changes may be made without checking again (to restart the approval process again) 


Do you have an idea or solution on how to possibly implement something like this?

Best regards

entry.title: Sending Email Campaigns to Account Team in SMC
entry.content:
Hi - Is it possible to send an email/SMS campaign to the Account Team Members of the Account?

I know we have a feature of "Send on Behalf Of" but the idea is besides sending the Account itself, we should also notify the Sales Rep of the Account via the same Campaign.

Cheers,

Marc

entry.title: Stop campaigns
entry.content:
Hello,

We are sending campaigns using the badi Open Channel to an external system, but within the flow of the operation we need to send them when a campaign is canceled, does anyone know how to do that in the code or if there is a way to send that a campaign is canceled?

Thank you

Nancy

SAP Marketing Cloud

entry.title: Adapt UI depending on user role
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

can i create an UI in dependency of an user role or marketing area? 

For example: Some Users should see more Tabs or Custom Fields in the Contacts-App than others. 

Thanks for your help!
Katharina

entry.title: Lead Scoring - Saving and Retention
entry.content:
Hi Community!

Could someone please explain how do Save and Retention Period work in the Score Builder App, more specifically where are scores saved?

I could find documentation around when the saving is done (daily at 12 pm, weekly on Sunday.. etc), but I dont understand where those scores are saved and where I can see the history of the changes within scores for certain users?

Also, how does Retention period work? What is the main purpose of this feature? I have one potential use case: what would happen if the Saving is set to monthly, but Retention period is 2 days?

Many thanks,
Cagri - On behalf of @PedjaStan

entry.title: What is the use of Retention Period for Scores
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Could you please explain what is the use of Retention Period for Scores

what will happen if i select

Scores Saved = Monthly

Retention Period for Scores = 2 days

Regards,

Amshu

entry.title: Segmentation Base Population Restrictions
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have a request to restrict the base population of the Contacts Segmentation profile.  The requirement is to filter out contacts who have not engaged within the last 12 months.  Additionally, the requirement is to focus only on certain interaction types, such as web visits, forms submissions and message response (opens, click-thrus) - basically positive interactions that demonstrate intent.

One option would be to use re-usable segmentation building blocks, but ideally we'd like to have the population pre-filtered so users do not need to do anything.  I've also looked at applying a filter to the profile within the Segmentation Configuration app, but not sure this will cover the full requirements.

If anyone has any advice on how to address this requirement I'd be greatful!

Regards,

Bradley

entry.title: Complete export of all customer data (Marketing Cloud)
entry.content:
I would like to export all my customers' contact information from the SAP Marketing Cloud. We are currently preparing the migration to a new tool and therefore need the information.
I was told that I can use the Customer Data Return app.
I can't currently find the right data source for the export.
Can anyone help me?
